summary: Taiwanese early-career candidate currently based in Fukuoka, Japan. Native Chinese speaker with JLPT N2 Japanese and practical English communication ability. Has experience in hotel front desk support, guest service, cafe operations, multilingual customer service, SNS/community management, event planning, and basic PC/data work. Also has an academic background in Information Management and is currently building JobFit-AI with Next.js as a personal AI-assisted development project.
longTermPositioning: A multilingual, Japan-based early-career professional who can connect people, places, cultures, and technology through hospitality, community building, SNS communication, and AI-assisted product development.

personalBackground.availability: Flexible for interviews, trials, and work start date

careerGoals: Build a stable foundation for living and working in Japan.
Gain practical Japanese workplace communication experience.
Turn multilingual ability into real value in hospitality, tourism, customer service, SNS, and community-related work.
Learn how welcoming spaces such as guesthouses, hostels, cafes, and community venues are operated.
Connect online community management experience with offline hospitality, events, and SNS/PR work.
Use real workplace experience to discover practical problems that can later be solved with AI, web tools, or product development.
Develop a unique career position combining multilingual communication, hospitality, community building, SNS operation, and AI-assisted web/product skills.

responsibilities: Supported front desk operations and guest reception.
Assisted with check-in and facility guidance.
Explained hotel facilities and usage rules to domestic and international guests.
Provided customer service in Japanese, English, and Chinese.
Worked in the hotel cafe, including drink preparation and simple latte art.
Supported kitchen tasks, facility organization, and cleaning.
highlights: Gained practical experience in multilingual hospitality service.
Learned to communicate clearly and politely with guests from different backgrounds.
Contributed to creating a comfortable and welcoming stay for guests.

responsibilities: Managed SNS and LINE OpenChat communities.
Handled participant communication and community moderation.
Organized community rules and maintained a positive atmosphere.
Supported online community growth and engagement.
highlights: Grew a LINE OpenChat community from 0 to around 3,000 members.
Developed experience in community atmosphere building.
Practiced communication, moderation, and basic content operation.

responsibilities: Completed mandatory military service in Taiwan.

languages.notes: Able to handle practical customer service communication and daily workplace communication. Still improving advanced business Japanese.
Able to handle basic to intermediate customer service and guest communication.

certifications.name: JLPT N2
certifications.issuer: Japan Foundation / Japan Educational Exchanges and Services

certifications.name: Taiwan ordinary automobile driver's license
certifications.notes: Japanese translation obtained. Able to drive in Japan. Automatic transmission only.

projects.name: JobFit-AI
projects.description: A job tracking and AI job-fit analysis tool built with Next.js to support job search, application status tracking, and future AI-based job matching.
highlights: Built a home page job list and job detail pages.
Implemented persistent job application status updates using a PATCH API.
Added status badges and status-based filtering on the home page.
Practiced small-task development, manual testing, and clean Git commits.
Used AI coding tools to plan, implement, review, and debug features.
Currently preparing a user profile data source for future job-fit analysis.
projects.positioning: Main portfolio project for AI-assisted development and practical product-building ability.

projects.name: LINE OpenChat community operation
projects.description: Managed and grew an online community using LINE OpenChat.
highlights: Grew the community from 0 to around 3,000 members.
Created rules, managed posts, and supported participant communication.
Maintained a safe and positive atmosphere for members.
projects.positioning: Useful experience for hospitality, community, event, SNS, and customer-facing roles.

projects.name: Nihongo Bubble
projects.description: A Japanese learning assistant concept focused on contextual vocabulary and practical learning support.
highlights: Designed the concept for a Japanese learning support tool.
Explored how AI can assist with vocabulary, context, and study flow.
projects.positioning: Supports interest in language learning, AI tools, and product thinking.

rolePreferences: Guesthouse, hostel, or hotel roles using multilingual customer service
Cafe bar or reception roles where communication skills are valued
Inbound tourism roles involving Japanese, English, and Chinese
SNS operation assistant or PR assistant roles
Roles where practical project experience is valued
availabilityPreferences: Open to part-time, full-time, or W-work depending on conditions
avoid: Roles requiring deep backend ownership from day one
Roles requiring fluent business Japanese from day one without support
Highly ambiguous roles with no mentorship
Positions where language support or onboarding is insufficient

constraints: Needs roles that are realistic for an early-career candidate.
Japanese is JLPT N2 level and practical for many customer service situations, but advanced business Japanese is still a growth area.
Professional software engineering experience is limited; web development should be evaluated as junior or trainee level.
Backend, database, testing, and deployment experience should be considered growth areas.
For hospitality roles, the strongest match is multilingual guest support, hotel/cafe operations, reception, inbound tourism, event support, and SNS support.
For engineering roles, the strongest match is junior-friendly frontend or AI-assisted development roles where practical project experience is valued.
The candidate is looking for work that can support stable life in Japan while also building future career options.

highFit: Hotel, hostel, guesthouse, cafe, or reception roles that value multilingual customer service.
Inbound tourism roles involving Chinese, Japanese, and English communication.
Customer-facing roles where hospitality, clear explanation, and guest support are important.
SNS operation assistant, PR assistant, event assistant, or community support roles.
Roles where experience in hospitality, cafe work, SNS/community management, and event planning can be combined.
Roles that help the candidate gain practical Japanese workplace communication experience.
Roles that involve traveler interaction, local culture, community spaces, events, or welcoming atmosphere creation.
Junior-friendly frontend or web application roles using React, Next.js, TypeScript, or JavaScript.
Roles involving AI tools, productivity tools, internal tools, or product prototyping.
Teams open to candidates who use AI tools responsibly to improve productivity.
mediumFit: General customer service roles with moderate Japanese requirements.
Sales or retail roles focused on inbound customers or multilingual support.
Hotel roles requiring some night shifts or broader operations if training is available.
Cafe or restaurant roles where customer communication and atmosphere building are valued.
Full-stack roles with manageable backend requirements.
Frontend roles requiring some testing, deployment, or API integration.
Japanese workplace roles where language expectations are moderate or support is available.
Roles where SNS or event support is only a minor part of the job but still available.
lowFit: Senior frontend or senior full-stack roles.
Backend-heavy roles requiring strong database, infrastructure, or system design experience.
Roles requiring fluent business Japanese from day one.
Roles requiring many years of professional engineering experience.
Hospitality management roles requiring extensive management experience.
Sales roles with heavy individual quota pressure and little training.
Jobs with no clear training, no feedback, and no opportunity to develop communication or practical workplace skills.
Jobs that do not help the candidate build a stable foundation in Japan or future career options.

resumePositioning: For hospitality roles, emphasize multilingual customer service, hotel front desk support, cafe work, guest guidance, and creating a welcoming atmosphere.
For guesthouse, hostel, and cafe bar roles, emphasize interest in spaces where travelers and local people can interact.
For SNS, PR, and event assistant roles, emphasize SNS/community management, LINE OpenChat growth to around 3,000 members, photography, Canva, simple video editing, and event planning.
For inbound tourism or sales roles, emphasize native Chinese, JLPT N2 Japanese, English communication, and cross-cultural customer support.
For junior web roles, position JobFit-AI as the main portfolio project.
For web development applications, emphasize practical implementation: Next.js pages, API route, status persistence, UI state, and Git workflow.
Emphasize AI-assisted development as a productivity skill, not as a replacement for understanding.
Frame current gaps honestly as learning areas: backend, database, testing, deployment, advanced TypeScript, and advanced business Japanese.
Position the candidate as someone who can connect multilingual communication, hospitality, community operation, SNS content, and AI/web tool thinking.
interviewTalkingPoints: I can support guests in Chinese, Japanese, and English.
At Hotel Hakuba Goryu Base, I supported front desk work, facility guidance, cafe operations, and guest communication.
I care about explaining things clearly so guests can feel comfortable and safe.
I have experience growing and managing an online community from 0 to around 3,000 members.
I am interested in creating spaces where travelers, locals, and community members can interact comfortably.
I want to gain more practical Japanese workplace communication experience and build a stable foundation for working in Japan.
I have an Information Management background and am also building a Next.js job-fit analysis tool as a personal project.
I use AI tools to improve planning, learning, debugging, and productivity while continuing to understand the implementation myself.
In the future, I want to connect hospitality, multilingual communication, SNS/community experience, and AI/web tools.

hospitality: Interest in guesthouse, hostel, traveler community, and local exchange
SNS/photo/event support as an extra contribution
Desire to create a welcoming and comfortable atmosphere for guests
snsPrEvent: Ability to connect online communication with offline events or physical spaces
inboundSalesCustomerService: Native Chinese plus Japanese N2 and English communication
Experience explaining services to international guests
Basic PC and data entry ability
Interest in supporting travelers and international customers in Japan
juniorWebDeveloper: Next.js, React, TypeScript learning and implementation
Strong product thinking from real job-search pain point
Potential to build tools based on real hospitality, job search, language learning, or community problems
